subject: Re: hybrid data processing
body:
Hey Jesse,

It should be enough to have two separate instances of Warp for
preprocessing, one for your high dose images and one for your low dose
images. These can then be combined by copying the files from one into the
other. Everything downstream should then work as expected, the only thing
you will need to take care of is making sure the cumulative dose is dealt
with correctly, if it's read in from your mdocs you should be good to go :-)

Cheers,

Alister

On Thu, 16 Nov 2023 at 22:55, mrJ <bluufive@gmail.com> wrote:

> dear WARP commnunity,
>
> I'd like to try processing hybrid cryo-EM data: a single high-dose (HD)
> image and a typical tilt series.  The HD image has more frames, higher
> dose, and will be aligned with patches -- quite different than how the
> tilts are corrected. Are there any clever ideas on how I might implement
> these two data types in the WARP pipeline?  One option I can see is to
> align the HD image with Motioncorr outside of WARP, then maybe overwrite
> the WARP aligned micrograph in the "average" folder.  However I'm not sure
> how WARP will then deal with tomo/subtomo reconstruction -- for on-the-fly
> reconstructions does it use the frames or the averaged micrograph?  If the
> latter then I guess it just might work.   However.... if I try running M
> later on then I will certainly somehow need the frames too.  Perhaps for
> that I could simply generate a "fake" tilt movie by summing my frames (e.g.
> 40) into groups which would match the frames for a tilt (e.g. 8).  So I'd
> separate out 5 frames at a time, correct it in MotionCorr, do that 8 times,
> then create a stack with 8 micrographs.  I'd have to also correct the pixel
> intensity but that's a different problem, for now just trying to
> incorporate it into WARP.
>
> thanks!
>
> Jesse
>
> --
